California
Fair Political
Practices Commlsslon
June 27, 1990
Mrs. H, Alfred Dunn
california Federation RePublican women
323a E. Villa Knolls Drive
Pasadena, cA 
91107
Re: Your Request For Advice
Our Fite No. A-9o-379
Dear Mrs. Dunn:
You have requested advice on behalf of the California
Federation of Republican wonen undef .the canpaign provisions
the Political 
Reform Act (the Act).1/
QUESTION
For purposes of the Act's contribution limitations, are
Federation clubs rrpersonsrr or itpolitical 
comnitteesrr?
Of
the
CONCLUSION
If a Federation club meets the definition of a recipient
cornmittee, it will be either a itpolitical 
committeerr or a rrbroad
based political 
committeerr for purposes of the contribution
Iinitations.
FACTS
The Federation receives membership dues which are used to
accornptish the Federation's goals of inforning the public through
education and activity. 
Funds are not raised for the purpose of
making campaign contributions. 
However, some of the Federationrs
clubs do make contributions fron the dues paynents they receive.
covernment Code
references are to the
Comrnission regulations
Section 18000, et ses.
Titl,e 2, Division 6 of
Sections 81000-91015. AI1 statutory
Government Code unLess otherwise indicated.
appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
AII references to regulations are to
the California Code of Regulations.
'128 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8t'! 7 o Sacramenro CA 95ttO4-OgO7 o (916) j22-566tr


!Irs. H. Alfred Dunn
Page Tero
ANALYSIS
A nembership organization, such as the Federation clubs,
meets the detinition-of a rrrecipient cornmitteert under the Act if
it receives contributions totalling $I,ooo or more during a
calendar year. (Section 82013 (a) . )
Section 82015 defines ttcontributi-on, rr and Regulation I8215
further explains the types of payments that meet the definition of
"contributions. I'
With regard to membership dues, or other receipts that are
intended for a purpose other than making campaign contri.butions or
expenditures, Regulation 18215 provides that such pa)rnents are
"clntributions,, if they are rrearrnarkedrr for making contributions
or expenditures. Regulation 18215 provides that a paynent is
trearmarkedrr when:
At the time of making the palaoent, the donor knows
or has reason to know that the pa)ment or funds with
which the payment will be conmingled will be used to
make contrlbutions ... or expenditures. . . .
When an organization, such as the Federation or the
individual Fedeiation c1ubs, uses nembership dues to nake campaiqn
contributions or expenditures, the portion of the dues which is
used to make contributions or expenditures is deened to be
rrearmarkedrr once the members are on notice that a portion of their
dues may be used for political purposes. l{embers are deemed to be
on notice that a portion of their dues rnay be used for making
canpaign contributions or expenditures if the organization has
used the funds for that purpose in a previous year. The enclosed
infornation sheet entitled rrorganizations Which Are Formed
erinarily For Purposes other Than uaking Contributions And
Expenditures To Support or oppose California State or Local
candidatest' explains this in detail.
A club which qual.if ies as a recipient comnittee must
determine lrhether it is a rrpolitical committeerr or a rrbroad based
political 
committeerr for purposes of the contribution limitations.
:iPo■itica■ committee'' means:
A collmittee of persons who receives contri―
IIti:1:efモ°
lalと: ::nt:I:u:::員:nE。 ::n:II:モ:s.
(SeCtiOn 85■02(c).)


Mrs. H. Alfred Dunn
Page Three
rrBroad based politicaL conmitteel means:
A committee of persons which has been in
existence for more than six months, receives
contributions from one hundred or more persons,
and acting in concert makes contributions to
five or more candidates.
(Section 85102 (d) . )
The enclosed trlnformation llanual on Canpaign Disclosure
Provisions of The Political Reforn Act For General Purpose
Recipient Committeesrr sets out the linitations 
on the amount of
contiibutions which may be made and wtrich may be received by
political 
committees and broad based political 
connittees-
The Act's linitations 
do not apply to contributions received
by a political 
cornmittee or a broad based political 
conmittee when
sirch Lontributions are used for Purposes other than naking
contributions to candidates. If a conmittee wishes to receive
contributions above the lirnits for this purpose, a separate bank
account must be established. It is not necessary for a cornmittee
to establish a separate campaign account unless contributions
which exceed the limitations are received. Enclosed is a copy of
Regulation 18535 which sets forth the conditions under which a
conruittee may accept contributions in excess of the Act's
contribution- linits. 
2 /
Please call ne at (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions.
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
qr4,^r"rr-fu--t/
By: Jeanne Pritchard
chief, Technical Assistance
And Analysis Division
Enc losures
Regulation 18535 was adopted by the Commission in December
1989. Though it has not yet been subnitted to the Office of
Adrninistrative Law, it is an expression of Cornrnission policy. 
We
expect to subnit the reg'ulation to the Office of Adninistrative
Law within the next few weeks.
